refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly state the performance metric (link availability) and the evaluation period clarifies the requirement for design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime statistics over the evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and report link failures promptly.
FR3: Recover or switch to backup links to maintain availability.
FR4: Log link availability data for verification.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.
DP2: Real-time link health monitoring and alerting system.
DP3: Link status logging and analytics module.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.

coupling_check.notes: Redundancy and failover logic is modular and can be implemented independently from monitoring and logging, minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of multiple network paths or technologies for redundancy.
Risk that failover timing may impact perceived availability if not optimized.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating monitoring (DP2) tightly with failover (DP1) to improve responsiveness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Responsible for managing teleoperation link availability including redundancy and failover mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connections managed for redundancy and failover.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Send link status and availability metrics to NGHE Solution and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receive commands related to link management and failover control.

module_containment_scores.notes: High diffusion risk if link management is spread across NGHE Solution; containment is low.
Vehicle module has limited direct control over network links; poor containment.
Best containment and lowest diffusion risk; aligns with teleoperation link management responsibility.
Safety Services consumes link status but does not manage link availability directly.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing teleoperation link availability management within the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves ownership clarity.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on network hardware outside Teleoperation module control.
Coordination with Safety Services for alerts may introduce interface complexity.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts with Safety Services and NGHE Solution to manage dependencies.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (redundant network links with failover) directly addresses the refined FR sub-functions and meets availability targets.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk by assigning DP to Teleoperation module.
Interface complexity with Safety Services and NGHE Solution noted but manageable.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module provides strong containment for link availability functionality.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and link availability logs is feasible within Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Enhance interface definitions between Teleoperation and Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.
Consider integration of monitoring and logging functions in future refinement levels.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement of monitoring and logging sub-FRs and their DP/IM assignments is needed to fully cover the availability requirement.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and report link failures promptly.
FR3: Recover or switch to backup links to maintain availability.
FR4: Log link availability data for verification.

revise.why: To decompose the availability requirement into atomic sub-FRs for better DP and IM mapping.

assumptions: Multiple network links are available for redundancy.
Teleoperation module has control over network failover mechanisms.
open_risks: Failover timing may impact perceived availability.
Interface complexity with Safety Services for safety alerts.
evidence_needed: Network link redundancy capabilities and failover performance data.
Telemetry data format and availability for verification.
